steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence states that regionally popular sports appeared as “onetime showcases” at past Olympics. The second sentence gives specific examples that illustrate exactly that pattern: kaatsen appeared only once (1928), while internationally popular handball became permanent. The transition must signal that the examples logically follow from the rule just stated.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Moreover,’: Adds new information rather than showing that what follows is a result or illustration of what came before. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Accordingly,’: Means “as a result” or “in keeping with this,” correctly signaling that the examples are a direct consequence of the pattern established in the first sentence. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Granted,’: Introduces a concession — acknowledging a point that weakens the writer’s argument — which does not fit here. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘However,’: Signals contrast or contradiction, but the second sentence reinforces, not contradicts, the first. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
